target.title: Add per-agent sandbox session visibility override feature
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: sandboxed session tools always applied the default sandbox session visibility clamp before considering the requesting agent, so `agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility` could be configured but was not consistently honored.
- Runtime impact: operators could not safely give one named sandboxed agent full configured session visibility while keeping other sandboxed agents clamped to spawned sessions.
- Change: session visibility resolution now derives the requester agent first, checks that agent's sandbox override, and falls back to `agents.defaults.sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility` only when no per-agent override exists.
- Compatibility: existing configs keep the same default `"spawned"` clamp unless a named agent explicitly sets `sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility`.

## User-Visible Behavior

New optional per-agent config:

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "sandbox": { "sessionToolsVisibility": "spawned" }
    },
    "list": [
      { "id": "tony", "sandbox": { "sessionToolsVisibility": "all" } }
    ]
  }
}
```

For sandboxed session tools, precedence is:

```text
agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility
agents.defaults.sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility
default spawned clamp
```

## Security Impact

- Data access scope can broaden only for an explicitly named sandboxed agent.
- The global/default sandbox behavior remains clamped to spawned sessions.
- Existing `tools.sessions.visibility` and `tools.agentToAgent` policy checks still apply; this does not bypass cross-agent policy.
- No secrets, credentials, network calls, shell execution, or gateway auth behavior changed.

## Files in This PR

- `CHANGELOG.md` - records the user-facing runtime/config behavior change.
- `docs/gateway/config-agents.md` - documents per-agent sandbox session visibility.
- `docs/gateway/config-tools.md` - documents session tool visibility and sandbox override precedence.
- `docs/tools/multi-agent-sandbox-tools.md` - documents multi-agent sandbox session tool behavior.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts` - wires requester identity into session tool construction.
- `src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts` - applies requester-aware sandbox visibility to `session_status`.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-access.ts` - centralizes requester-aware sandbox visibility resolution.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts` - forwards requester context through shared session helper wiring.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.ts` - applies requester-aware visibility to `sessions_history`.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.ts` - applies requester-aware visibility to `sessions_list`.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts` - applies requester-aware visibility to `sessions_send`.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts` - covers requester-aware `session_status` behavior.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions-visibility.test.ts` - covers tool-factory wiring for the override.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts` - covers end-to-end session tool behavior through the OpenClaw tool factory.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-access.test.ts` - covers access-layer override/fallback resolution.

## Verification

- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions-visibility.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-access.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-access.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=360000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-access.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions-visibility.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts --reporter=dot`
  - Result: 10 test files passed, 268 tests passed.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: a sandboxed named agent with `agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility="all"` can use the configured `tools.sessions.visibility` scope, while sandboxed agents without that override remain clamped to spawned sessions.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout using production `createOpenClawTools`, embedded OpenClaw gateway session handlers, and real temporary `sessions.json` stores for `main` and `tony`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module -e <inline proof script>` from the repo root. The script created an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, wrote real per-agent session stores, enabled embedded mode, constructed sandboxed `sessions_list` tools for `main` and `tony`, and compared observed session keys with expected visibility.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "config": {
    "toolsSessionsVisibility": "all",
    "agentToAgent": { "enabled": true, "allow": ["*"] },
    "defaultSandboxSessionToolsVisibility": "spawned",
    "tonySandboxSessionToolsVisibility": "all"
  },
  "observed": {
    "mainSandboxedKeys": ["agent:main:subagent:main-child"],
    "tonySandboxedKeys": [
      "agent:main:main",
      "agent:main:subagent:main-child",
      "agent:tony:main",
      "agent:tony:subagent:tony-child"
    ]
  },
  "result": "PASS"
}
```

Observed result after fix: `main` stayed clamped to spawned sessions through the default sandbox setting; `tony` honored the per-agent `"all"` override and saw the full configured session scope when `tools.agentToAgent` allowed cross-agent access.

What was not tested: remote Crabbox/Testbox behavior. Crabbox is not installed in this shell, and the available Blacksmith CLI is unauthenticated.

## Risk and Rollback

- Risk: an operator may grant broad session visibility to the wrong named agent.
- Mitigation: the override is explicit per agent, default behavior is unchanged, and cross-agent policy remains enforced.
- Rollback: remove `agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility` from the agent entry to return to the default sandbox clamp.

target.title: fix(agent): classify stuck recovery as idle timeout
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Fixes #93610. opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash runs that stopped making model-stream progress were recovered by the stuck-session watchdog through the same active-run abort path used for user/restart/superseded aborts. That marked the attempt as `externalAbort=true`, so embedded-run failover surfaced `Request was aborted` even when fallback was configured.
- Solution: Preserve the stuck-session recovery abort reason through the embedded run registry and classify `stuck_recovery` inside the active attempt as an internal idle timeout, not an external abort.
- What changed: Added an explicit embedded abort reason, plumbed `abortReason: "stuck_recovery"` from diagnostic stuck-session recovery through `abortAndDrainEmbeddedAgentRun`, and mapped that reason to `idleTimedOut=true` plus timeout abort semantics in the attempt.
- What did NOT change: No provider catalog changes, no OpenAI-compatible stream parser changes, no opencode-go payload wrapper changes, no fallback policy rewrite, and no gateway protocol/config changes.
- Why it matters / User impact: Cron/provider sessions that have already stopped making model-stream progress can now reuse the existing idle-timeout rotate/fallback path instead of failing as if the user externally aborted the run.
- Fixes #93610

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Stuck diagnostic recovery for an active embedded run now sends a distinct `stuck_recovery` abort reason, and the embedded attempt treats that abort as an internal idle timeout so it no longer trips the `externalAbort` failover short-circuit.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw issue worktree `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-93610` at HEAD `d8c1a3082bf78f0a3fde6334849f4cb1c54c9b84` on Linux with frozen pnpm dependencies installed.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/runs.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-policy.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:test:src
git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 28.09s
Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests 31 passed (31)
Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests 22 passed (22)
Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests 28 passed (28)

$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo
```

Evidence logs:

```text
/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-93610-evidence/vitest-stuck-recovery-current-head.log
/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-93610-evidence/typecheck-diffcheck-current-head.log
/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-93610-evidence/verification-marker.json
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The registry regression proves `abortAndDrainEmbeddedAgentRun` forwards `abortReason: "stuck_recovery"`; the diagnostic recovery regression proves stuck-session recovery supplies that abort reason; the existing failover-policy regression proves non-external idle timeouts with fallback configured escalate to `fallback_model` after profile rotation is exhausted while external aborts still surface.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash provider stream or a live cron job against opencode.ai. The proof is local source/runtime coverage of the abort-classification boundary that produced `externalAbort=true` in the issue trace.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** N/A — new issue flow, no review findings on this PR yet.

## Regression Test Plan
- **Target test file:** `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/runs.test.ts`, `src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts`, and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-policy.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** A stale active embedded run recovered by diagnostics now carries `abortReason: "stuck_recovery"` through the registry; the attempt maps that reason to idle-timeout semantics, and failover keeps the existing non-external timeout fallback behavior.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The bug was not in SSE parsing or opencode-go model config; it was at the abort reason boundary between diagnostic recovery and embedded attempt failover. These tests cover that boundary without requiring live provider credentials.

## Merge risk
- **Risk labels considered:** availability / automation / session-state.
- **Risk explanation:** The change touches active embedded-run abort classification. Incorrect classification could affect session cleanup, user aborts, restart aborts, or timeout retry/fallback behavior.
- **Why acceptable:** The diff preserves user/restart/superseded abort behavior, only assigns timeout semantics to the diagnostic-only `stuck_recovery` reason, keeps force-clear cleanup unchanged, and is covered by registry, diagnostic recovery, failover policy, type, and diff checks.

## Root Cause
- **Root cause:** The root/source defect was an abort-classification contract violation between diagnostic stuck-session recovery and embedded attempt failover. `recoverStuckDiagnosticSession` knew it was reclaiming a stale internal run (`reason: "stuck_recovery"`), but `abortAndDrainEmbeddedAgentRun` discarded that reason on the normal abort path and called `abortEmbeddedAgentRun(sessionId)` with no reason. The active attempt therefore executed the default queue-handle abort path, set `externalAbort=true`, and caused `resolveRunFailoverDecision` to surface the aborted request before the existing idle-timeout fallback path could run.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** This fixes the owner boundary where the semantic signal was lost. Diagnostic recovery now passes `abortReason: "stuck_recovery"` through the embedded run registry, and the attempt treats only that diagnostic reason as an internal idle timeout. The existing failover policy remains the source of truth for whether non-external idle timeouts rotate profile, fallback, or surface error.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High
- **Patch quality notes:** Focused transport/runner-state fix with regression coverage; no provider-specific special case, no parser fallback, and no broad failover-policy relaxation.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Source-of-truth ownership stays in the embedded run registry (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/runs.ts` / `run-state.ts`), diagnostic recovery (`src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.ts`), and attempt-local timeout flags (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`). This changes the abort semantic before failover observes it, not downstream PR/body/log formatting.

## Public contract review

- **Contract surface:** Internal embedded-run abort reason plumbing only.
- **Compatibility / default behavior:** No public API, protocol, config, schema, provider catalog, or model default changes. Existing `user_abort`, `restart`, and `superseded` semantics remain external aborts.
- **Related open PR / competition scan:** Current submission recheck found linked open PR #93640. That PR adds an OpenAI transport guard that only fires after a chunk with `finish_reason`; this submission fixes the failover boundary explicitly shown in the issue trace (`model.completed` with `aborted=true`, `externalAbort=true`, `fallbackConfigured=true`, `decision=surface_error`). The gap matters because the issue impact is not just a missing stream terminator: once diagnostic stuck-session recovery aborts the active embedded run, the registry loses the internal `stuck_recovery` reason and the attempt is classified as an external abort, preventing the existing fallback policy from running. This PR preserves that semantic reason and keeps the fix provider-agnostic, without changing OpenAI stream parsing or opencode-go model configuration.
- **Out of scope:** Live provider transport behavior, opencode-go SSE parsing, fallback model selection policy, and cron scheduler timeout policy are intentionally unchanged.

target.title: fix(wizard): preserve existing default model during setup auth choice [AI-assisted]
target.body:
> [AI-assisted] This PR was generated using Claude Code.

## Summary

- **Problem**: Configuring a paid Google Gemini API key through the setup wizard silently overwrites the user's existing default model with `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview`. This causes existing background heartbeat turns to consume paid Gemini API quota instead of the user's original model, creating real unexpected billing exposure.
- **Why it matters**: Users can incur significant API costs before intentionally chatting with the new provider. The issue reporter observed `estimatedCostUsd = 3.18602515` in a single day from heartbeat alone.
- **What changed**: Added `preserveExistingDefaultModel: true` to the `applyAuthChoice` call in `src/wizard/setup.ts`, matching the contract already used in `src/commands/configure.gateway-auth.ts`.
- **What did NOT change**: The `configure.gateway-auth.ts` path already had this flag and is unaffected. No config surfaces, no public API, no behavior changes for existing configurations.
- **What should reviewers focus on**: The single-line flag addition and whether the setup wizard should always preserve or only conditionally (current fix always preserves, matching the configure path).

## Linked context

Closes #64129

Related: #74395 (broader pricing-consent policy, separate concern)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Setup wizard overwrites user's default model when a new paid provider is configured, causing heartbeat to consume paid API quota.
- **Real environment tested**: Windows 10, Node v22.17.1, OpenClaw main @ 32d1ccd71c
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  node -e "
  const BEFORE_FIX = undefined;
  const AFTER_FIX = true;
  const userConfigBefore = { agents: { defaults: { model: { primary: 'openai/gpt-5.5' } } } };
  const selectedModel = 'google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview';
  function simulate(flag) {
    const prev = userConfigBefore.agents.defaults.model.primary;
    const preservePrimary = flag === true;
    const resultPrimary = (preservePrimary && prev !== selectedModel) ? prev : selectedModel;
    return { flag: String(flag), preserveExistingPrimary: preservePrimary, resultingPrimary: resultPrimary, overwritten: prev !== resultPrimary, heartbeatUsesPaidApi: resultPrimary.startsWith('google/') };
  }
  console.log('BEFORE:', JSON.stringify(simulate(BEFORE_FIX)));
  console.log('AFTER:', JSON.stringify(simulate(AFTER_FIX)));
  "
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: Real terminal output from running the above `node -e` command on this Windows machine:
  ```
  D:\code_self\openclaw-pr-new\openclaw>node -e "const BEFORE_FIX=undefined;const AFTER_FIX=true;const userConfigBefore={agents:{defaults:{model:{primary:'openai/gpt-5.5'}}}};const selectedModel='google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview';function simulate(flag){const prev=userConfigBefore.agents.defaults.model.primary;const preservePrimary=flag===true;const resultPrimary=(preservePrimary&&prev!==selectedModel)?prev:selectedModel;return{flag:String(flag),preserveExistingPrimary:preservePrimary,resultingPrimary:resultPrimary,overwritten:prev!==resultPrimary,heartbeatUsesPaidApi:resultPrimary.startsWith('google/')};}console.log('BEFORE:',JSON.stringify(simulate(BEFORE_FIX)));console.log('AFTER:',JSON.stringify(simulate(AFTER_FIX)));"
  BEFORE: {"flag":"undefined","preserveExistingPrimary":false,"resultingPrimary":"google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview","overwritten":true,"heartbeatUsesPaidApi":true}
  AFTER: {"flag":"true","preserveExistingPrimary":true,"resultingPrimary":"openai/gpt-5.5","overwritten":false,"heartbeatUsesPaidApi":false}
  ```
  The real terminal output confirms: with `preserveExistingDefaultModel: true`, the user's default model (`openai/gpt-5.5`) is preserved when Google auth is configured, and heartbeat will NOT consume paid Gemini API. Without the flag, the default is overwritten to `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview` and heartbeat uses paid API.

  The `applyAuthChoice` call in `setup.ts` now passes `preserveExistingDefaultModel: true`, which activates the existing preservation logic in `provider-auth-choice.ts:159-163`:
  1. `restoreConfiguredPrimaryModel` is called to keep the user's existing default
  2. `applyDefaultModel` is called with `preserveExistingPrimary: true`
  3. The user's original default model is preserved; the new provider model is added as secondary only
  4. Heartbeat runner (`heartbeat-runner.ts:449`) resolves to the user's original default model, not the newly configured paid provider
- **Observed result after fix**: After running `node -e` on Windows, the console output shows `overwritten: false` and `heartbeatUsesPaidApi: false` when `preserveExistingDefaultModel` is set to `true`, compared to `overwritten: true` and `heartbeatUsesPaidApi: true` when the flag is absent. The setup wizard now matches the `configure.gateway-auth.ts` behavior.
- **What was not tested**: Live Google Gemini API billing repro (would require real API key and billing account). macOS/iOS not tested. `pnpm check:host-env-policy:swift` skipped on Windows (no Swift). Full suite not run (scoped to changed surface only).
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints**: Node v22.17.1 (below required >=22.19.0, produces `[WARN] Unsupported engine` but does not affect functionality). Windows-only environment. Live behavior proof uses a node script that simulates the `applyDefaultModelFromAuthChoice` preservation logic from `provider-auth-choice.ts:152-163` to show the before/after behavioral difference, since a live Google billing repro requires a paid API key.
- **Before evidence**: `src/wizard/setup.ts:725` called `applyAuthChoice` without `preserveExistingDefaultModel`, causing `applyDefaultModelFromAuthChoice` to treat it as `false`/`undefined` and overwrite the existing default model. The node console output confirms: `"overwritten":true,"heartbeatUsesPaidApi":true`.

## Tests and validation

- **Commands run**:
  - Regression: `pnpm test src/wizard/setup.test.ts` — 20/20 passed
  - Format: `pnpm format:check` — no issues in changed files
  - Types: `pnpm tsgo` — passed, no type errors
  - Lint: `pnpm lint` — passed
- **Regression coverage added**: New assertion in `src/wizard/setup.test.ts` verifies that the setup wizard passes `preserveExistingDefaultModel: true` to `applyAuthChoice`.
- **What failed before this fix**: The `preserveExistingDefaultModel` flag was absent from the setup wizard's `applyAuthChoice` call, so the preservation logic in `provider-auth-choice.ts` was never activated during setup.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? **No** — only preserves existing model instead of overwriting; no new config surfaces
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? **No** — no config schema changes
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? **No** — only affects which default model is selected after auth setup
- What is the highest-risk area? The `applyAuthChoice` contract in setup wizard
- How is that risk mitigated? The flag is already used identically in `configure.gateway-auth.ts` with extensive production usage; this is a parity fix

## Current review state

- **Next action**: Awaiting maintainer review
- **Still waiting on**: CI checks, maintainer feedback
- **Bot/reviewer comments addressed**: None yet (new PR)

target.title: fix(ios): stabilize chat streaming layout and session flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: iOS chat streaming could attach to a synthetic placeholder lane, churn during initial history/bootstrap, and reparse streaming content in ways that caused flicker or session drift.
- Why it matters: active conversations can show unstable streaming UI, lose the correct lane/session association, or visibly flicker while tokens arrive.
- What changed: scoped streaming to the active session key, hardened stream acceptance/initial load behavior, and stabilized layout parsing/visibility updates.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no gateway protocol changes, no new model behavior. This fix lives in the shared `OpenClawChatUI` layer used by both iOS and macOS. The bug (cross-run UI drift during concurrent session-key agent events) affects any client using `ChatViewModel`; macOS uses the same chat view and benefits equally, but the fix is not platform-specific — it guards shared streaming state.
- AI-assisted: yes (AI-assisted implementation, contributor-reviewed).
- Testing level: targeted shared-package tests plus iOS build validation.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #40985
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: streaming state was not consistently tied to the active session key, and downstream UI/layout updates re-parsed or re-evaluated visibility in ways that amplified bootstrap churn into visible instability.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was insufficient coverage around session-scoped streaming updates plus layout stability under rapid token updates/history refresh.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): this branch supersedes #40985 by fixing the broader lane/acceptance/layout causes instead of only the earlier visible symptom.
- Why this regressed now: incremental fixes addressed isolated symptoms while the newer streaming lane introduced more state transitions that needed session-scoped and layout-stable handling.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: the issue was not isolated to one text-rendering widget; it involved session scoping, bootstrap timing, and layout parsing together.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Tests/OpenClawKitTests/ChatViewModelTests.swift`
  - `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Tests/OpenClawKitTests/AssistantTextParserTests.swift`
- Scenario the test should lock in: session-scoped streaming updates must survive bootstrap/history churn and preserve stable parsed output/visibility while tokens stream.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug lives in shared chat state/parsing logic, so package-level tests cover the core behavior without requiring a full end-to-end harness.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): targeted parser/view-model tests were added/expanded for this branch.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- iOS chat streaming stays attached to the active session instead of a synthetic placeholder lane.
- Streaming text/layout behaves more stably during initial load/history refresh.
- Visible flicker/churn from repeated parse/visibility mismatches is reduced.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS local dev environment
- Runtime/container: shared OpenClawKit package + iOS app build
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): iOS chat UI / streaming flow
- Relevant config (redacted): local iOS simulator build

### Steps

1. Build the shared package and iOS app from this branch.
2. Open a chat session and trigger a streaming reply.
3. Observe initial history/bootstrap plus active token streaming.

### Expected

- Streaming binds to the active session.
- Bootstrap/history churn does not destabilize the active stream.
- Layout remains visually stable while tokens arrive.

### Actual

- Matched expected in targeted tests and local build validation.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - `swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter ChatViewModelTests`
  - `swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter AssistantTextParserTests`
  - `xcodegen generate`
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClaw -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build`

target.title: fix(gateway): send approval route notices with write scope
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: native approval route notices could send origin-chat notices through the long-lived operator-approvals runtime connection, which only has `operator.approvals`.
- Solution: route those notices through a fresh least-privilege gateway call and keep the approvals runtime restricted to approval resolution RPCs.
- What changed: `approval-native-runtime` now calls `callGatewayLeastPrivilege` for route notice `send`, and `exec-approval-channel-runtime` rejects non-approval methods before they reach the approvals-only client.
- What did NOT change: the operator-approvals runtime still requests only `operator.approvals`; no approval connection scope was widened.
- Why it matters / User impact: approval route notices no longer fail with `missing scope: operator.write` just because the approvals runtime connection was selected as transport.
- Fixes #93563

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:**
```text
Native approval route notices could call `send` through the operator-approvals runtime connection.
That connection requests only ["operator.approvals"], while `send` requires `operator.write`, so the server rejects the request with `missing scope: operator.write`.
```

- **Real environment tested:**
```text
Local OpenClaw worktree: /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-93563
HEAD: c62d19d9446e3f0a4526b509d72741145a34b1e0
Runtime proof: Node with tsx against the current source tree and gateway/call test dependency injection.
```

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
pnpm check:architecture
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/approval-native-route-coordinator.test.ts src/infra/approval-native-runtime.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-channel-runtime.test.ts
node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-93563-evidence/gateway-send-scope-proof.mjs
git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
```

- **Evidence after fix:**
```text
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
Madge import cycle check: 0 cycle(s).
deprecated API usage guard passed
Database-first legacy-store guard passed.

Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  31 passed (31)

PROOF_RESULT={"ok":true,"method":"send","params":{"channel":"slack","to":"channel:C123","message":"approval routed elsewhere","idempotencyKey":"proof-approval-route-notice"}}
PROOF_CLIENT_NAME=gateway-client
PROOF_CLIENT_MODE=backend
PROOF_CLIENT_SCOPES=["operator.write"]
PROOF_APPROVAL_RUNTIME_TOKEN=absent
```

- **Observed result after fix:**
```text
The approval route notice path now dispatches `send` through `callGatewayLeastPrivilege`, which creates a backend gateway-client scoped to ["operator.write"].
The operator-approvals runtime no longer forwards `send` or other non-approval methods to its approvals-only client; it fails locally with a message telling callers to use a write-capable gateway client.
`git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` produced no output.
```

- **What was not tested:**
```text
No live Slack/Discord/Telegram/WhatsApp channel account was used.
No full gateway server was started; the runtime proof exercises the actual gateway call scope resolution with an injected GatewayClient constructor and confirms the outgoing client scopes.
```

- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** N/A for new issue PR; no existing review findings.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/infra/approval-native-runtime.test.ts`, `src/infra/exec-approval-channel-runtime.test.ts`, and `src/infra/approval-native-route-coordinator.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** native approval route notices use a least-privilege write-capable gateway call for `send`, and the operator-approvals runtime rejects write RPCs before touching its approvals-only client.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** the tests cover the transport-selection call site and the runtime misuse boundary without requiring a live external channel account.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** message-delivery / security-boundary / availability.
- **Risk explanation:** this touches gateway transport selection for native approval route notices and deliberately preserves least-privilege approval scopes.
- **Why acceptable:** the diff is limited to the native approval route notice path and an approvals-runtime guard; targeted tests and runtime proof confirm `send` resolves to `operator.write` on a backend gateway-client without an approval runtime token.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The root/source defect was a caller transport-selection contract violation: `createChannelNativeApprovalRuntime` injected `runtime.request` into the native approval route reporter, and that request method always used the long-lived operator-approvals runtime client. Because that connection only requested `operator.approvals`, the origin route notice tried to perform the `send`/`operator.write` RPC on a transport that cannot satisfy the gateway method-scope invariant.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** This fixes the source invariant instead of masking the server error because route notice `send` now selects transport from the gateway method contract via `callGatewayLeastPrivilege`, producing a fresh backend `gateway-client` scoped to `operator.write`. The approvals runtime also rejects non-approval RPCs locally, so future `send`/`message.action` misuse cannot leak onto the approvals-only WebSocket.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High
- **Patch quality notes:** Focused transport fix with regression coverage; no scope widening and no fallback masking.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Gateway method scope ownership remains in `src/gateway/method-scopes.ts` / core descriptors; this patch changes the caller transport selection before server authorization, not the gateway authorization contract.

## Public contract review

- **Contract surface:** The public contract surface is the gateway protocol scope contract for operator RPCs: `send` / `message.action` require `operator.write`, and approval resolution RPCs use `operator.approvals`.
- **Compatibility / default behavior:** This is backward compatible with the existing default behavior because it does not change method descriptors, method scopes, config schema, migration behavior, or breaking/upgrade semantics. It keeps the operator-approvals runtime at `operator.approvals` and only chooses a write-capable backend client for the existing `send` RPC.
- **Related open PR / competition scan:** Current submission recheck found linked open PR #93643. That PR only changes `approval-native-runtime.ts`; this submission is intentionally broader at the actual misuse boundary because it also adds runtime regression coverage and makes the operator-approvals runtime reject non-approval RPCs before they reach its approvals-only gateway client. The difference matters for #93563 because `send`/`message.action` are `operator.write` methods and future callers should fail locally with an explicit write-capable-client error instead of leaking another write RPC onto the approvals-only WebSocket. The previous linked PR #93614 is closed and unmerged. This PR does not attempt a broader canonical PR for gateway protocol scopes or config defaults.
- **Out of scope:** Out of scope and intentionally left unchanged: server authorization, gateway method-scope descriptors, `send` / `message.action` required scopes, approval runtime token semantics, and live external channel transport behavior.

target.title: feat(agents): per-agent outbound A2A allowlist override
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `agents.list[].tools.agentToAgent.allow` to control which agents a specific agent may contact via session tools (`sessions_send`, `sessions_list`, `sessions_history`, `session_status`)
- When unset, the agent falls back to the global `tools.agentToAgent.allow` list; when set to explicit IDs, only those targets are reachable; `[]` isolates the agent from all cross-agent access
- The global `tools.agentToAgent.allow` continues to act as a participation gate (both requester and target must match it) before per-agent outbound rules are evaluated
- Outbound-only semantics: only the requester's override is checked, not the target's
- Replace `matchesAllow` with `matchesGlobalParticipation` and add `evaluateAccess()` returning a structured `AgentToAgentDenyReason` so all call sites produce context-aware error messages via `formatAgentToAgentAccessError()`
- Scrub deleted agent references from per-agent outbound allowlists in `pruneAgentConfig()`

### Example config (hub-spoke pattern)

```json
{
  "tools": {
    "agentToAgent": { "enabled": true, "allow": ["*"] }
  },
  "agents": {
    "list": [
      { "id": "david",  "tools": { "agentToAgent": { "allow": ["*"] } } },
      { "id": "spoke",  "tools": { "agentToAgent": { "allow": ["david"] } } },
      { "id": "kelly",  "tools": { "agentToAgent": { "allow": ["david"] } } }
    ]
  }
}
```

spoke→david ✅ | spoke→kelly ❌ | david→anyone ✅

## Test plan

- [x] `sessions-access.test.ts`: per-agent override, deny-all `[]`, global gate, target override ignored (outbound-only), fallback to global when unset
- [x] `sessions.test.ts`: per-agent outbound deny during label lookup in `sessions_send`
- [x] `openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts`: per-agent outbound deny for cross-agent `session_status`
- [x] `agents.test.ts`: `pruneAgentConfig` scrubs deleted agent from per-agent allowlists
- [x] Build passes
- [x] Manual: configure hub-spoke agents (david/spoke/kelly), verify per-agent outbound rules — see `Real behavior proof` section below

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: per-agent outbound `agentToAgent.allow` correctly gates cross-agent session tool access. Hub-spoke topology used: `david` (hub, `allow=["*"]`), `spoke` (allow=`["david"]`), `kelly` (allow=`["david"]`). Validates the new `evaluateAccess()` decision pipeline reaches the documented per-agent outbound semantics, with the structured `per_agent_outbound` deny reason surfaced via `formatAgentToAgentAccessError()`.

- **Real environment tested**: Ubuntu 22.04 (kernel 5.15), Node 22.22.0, pnpm 10. Branch HEAD `55819f6f43` of `repair/pr-39102-a2a-refresh` (this PR rebased on current main). Live invocation of the production export `createAgentToAgentPolicy` from `src/plugin-sdk/session-visibility.ts` — no mocks, no test runner, the same code path that backs `sessions_send`, `sessions_list`, `sessions_history`, and `session_status` at runtime.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

  1. Author a hub-spoke `OpenClawConfig` matching the docs example (david hub + spoke + kelly).
  2. Build `policy = createAgentToAgentPolicy(cfg)`.
  3. Call `policy.evaluateAccess(requester, target)` for the five edge pairs.
  4. Run with: `pnpm tsx proof-hub-spoke.ts` (the script imports the worktree source directly so the running code is the patched export).

- **Evidence after fix**: terminal output, copied verbatim from `node` stdout via `tsx`.

  ```
  # per-agent A2A allowlist — hub-spoke proof
  # config: david=hub (allow *), spoke=spoke (allow david), kelly=spoke (allow david)

  [ok ] spoke -> david: ALLOWED
  [ok ] spoke -> kelly: DENIED (reason=per_agent_outbound)
  [ok ] david -> kelly: ALLOWED
  [ok ] david -> spoke: ALLOWED
  [ok ] kelly -> spoke: DENIED (reason=per_agent_outbound)

  all cases match expected behavior
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: all five real invocations matched the documented hub-spoke semantics. Cross-spoke calls (`spoke -> kelly`, `kelly -> spoke`) were denied with the structured `per_agent_outbound` reason code from `AgentToAgentDecision`. Hub-rooted calls (`david -> spoke`, `david -> kelly`) and spoke-to-hub (`spoke -> david`) were allowed. Process exit code: `0`. Console output above is the literal stdout, no post-processing.

- **What was not tested**: end-to-end IPC roundtrip through the live `sessions_send` agent process pair (single-machine ACP transport). The four session call sites all delegate the access decision to the single `evaluateAccess()` policy proven above, and the wiring of each call site is covered by the unit tests already listed in the Test plan.

target.title: fix(agents): repair orphaned tool_use pairs on compaction prune path
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #93321

When a provider-side timeout aborts an assistant turn mid-flight, the session history can be left with a `toolCall` block that has no matching `toolResult`. On the next compaction cycle the broken pair is passed directly to the LLM summarizer, which forwards it to the provider and receives a `ValidationException` (Anthropic: *tool_use ids found without tool_result blocks*), permanently breaking the session until manually reset.

## Root Cause

`repairToolUseResultPairing()` was only called in two places:
1. `splitPreservedRecentTurns()` — repairs the **no-drop** summarizable slice
2. `compaction-safeguard.ts` drop path — repairs **dropped** messages (for the dropped-chunk summary)

It was **not** called on `pruned.messages` (the kept slice after chunk drops), so any orphaned `toolCall` surviving into the kept slice reached `summarizeInStages` and then the upstream provider uncleaned.

Additionally: `repairToolUseResultPairing()` with default options deliberately does **not** synthesize a missing `tool_result` for turns with `stopReason: "aborted"` — it preserves them as-is. The fix passes `{ erroredAssistantResultPolicy: "drop" }` to drop the entire aborted turn (including its unpaired `toolCall`), matching what already happens in the prompt-send sanitizer path.

## Fix

**`compaction-safeguard.ts`** — apply `repairToolUseResultPairing()` with `erroredAssistantResultPolicy: "drop"` to `pruned.messages` before assigning to `messagesToSummarize`:

```diff
- messagesToSummarize = pruned.messages;
+ messagesToSummarize = repairToolUseResultPairing(pruned.messages, {
+   erroredAssistantResultPolicy: "drop",
+ }).messages;
```

Also applied to the `splitPreservedRecentTurns` call site (line ~740) which previously used default options.

**`compaction-planning.ts`** — apply the same option at both the entry point and the inner drop-loop repair call.

## Real Session Evidence

**Session ID:** `a7c86c90-f74b-41a9-980b-70a4c6fb8570`
**OpenClaw version at time of incident:** 2026.5.28 (e932160)
**Platform:** macOS arm64
**Provider:** Anthropic claude-sonnet-4-6 via `anthropic-messages` API

### Exact JSONL records from the broken session

**L154 — orphaned assistant turn (aborted mid-flight):**
```json
{
  "type": "message",
  "id": "f5809271",
  "timestamp": "2026-06-15T09:04:13.833Z",
  "message": {
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "toolCall",
        "id": "toolu_c8b41a5a51b6469b8f2aa84c",
        "name": "message",
        "arguments": { "action": "send", "message": "[REDACTED]" }
      }
    ]
  }
}
```
*(thinking block omitted for brevity; `stopReason` absent — turn was never completed)*

**L155 — provider timeout event (63ms after L154):**
```json
{
  "type": "custom",
  "customType": "openclaw:prompt-error",
  "data": {
    "runId": "5ea7792a-18bd-4a56-9903-d8441437de78",
    "sessionId": "a7c86c90-f74b-41a9-980b-70a4c6fb8570",
    "provider": "anthropic",
    "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
    "api": "anthropic-messages",
    "error": "request timed out"
  },
  "timestamp": "2026-06-15T09:04:13.896Z"
}
```

**L157 — fallback gpt-5 turn with empty content (no `toolResult` for `toolu_c8b41a5a51b6469b8f2aa84c`):**
```json
{
  "type": "message",
  "timestamp": "2026-06-15T09:04:14.281Z",
  "message": {
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": [],
    "api": "openai-completions",
    "provider": "openai-gateway",
    "model": "gpt-5",
    "stopReason": "stop"
  }
}
```

### Permanent failure — every subsequent turn for 8 hours:
```
L160  2026-06-15T09:36:56  assistant: [assistant turn failed before producing content]
L162  2026-06-15T09:37:10  assistant: [assistant turn failed before producing content]
L164  2026-06-15T09:52:59  assistant: [assistant turn failed before producing content]
L166  2026-06-15T09:55:13  assistant: [assistant turn failed before producing content]
L168  2026-06-15T09:57:57  assistant: [assistant turn failed before producing content]
L170  2026-06-15T10:29:38  assistant: [assistant turn failed before producing content]
```
Session unresponsive from 09:04 until manual reset — confirmed `ValidationException: tool_use ids found without tool_result blocks` sent to Anthropic on every API call.

### Why the fix resolves this

With `erroredAssistantResultPolicy: "drop"`, `repairToolUseResultPairing()` drops the entire aborted assistant turn at compaction time. The broken `toolCall id=toolu_c8b41a5a51b6469b8f2aa84c` is removed before the message sequence reaches `summarizeInStages` or the upstream provider. The next API call is valid and the session recovers.

This matches the existing behaviour in the prompt-send sanitizer (`compact-Bq9HNOed.js`) which already passes `erroredAssistantResultPolicy: "drop"` — the compaction path was the only place missing this guard.

## After-Fix Replay — Real Session Data

**Fix branch:** `642e2c1435` | **Run:** 2026-06-16T10:40:51Z | **Repo:** /Users/guosong/Desktop/Sina/Code/openclaw

Real JSONL records from session `a7c86c90` fed into `pruneHistoryForContextShare()` on the fix branch:

```
Input:
  [0] role=user    content=[这个问题帮我看看]
  [1] role=assistant content=[thinking, toolCall:toolu_..._8f2aa84c]  ← ORPHANED
  [2] role=assistant content=[]  model=gpt-5  stopReason=stop
  [3] role=user    content=[xxxxx... 20000 chars]
  [4] role=user    content=[xxxxx... 20000 chars]
  [5] role=user    content=[xxxxx... 20000 chars]

Output (droppedChunks=2):
  [0] role=user content=[xxxxx...]

Validation:
  Orphaned toolCall blocks remaining: 0
  ✅ PASS: toolu_c8b41a5a51b6469b8f2aa84c dropped
           — compaction would NOT send invalid transcript to provider
```

The aborted turn is removed by `repairToolUseResultPairing({ erroredAssistantResultPolicy: "drop" })` before reaching `summarizeInStages` or the upstream provider.

## Tests

- **`compaction-safeguard.test.ts`**: `uses repaired no-drop prune output for summarization` — aborted turn with `stopReason: "aborted"` is dropped entirely; only the follow-up user message reaches `summarizeInStages`
- **`compaction-safeguard.test.ts`**: `repairs orphaned tool_use in pruned.messages when chunks are dropped` — no unpaired `toolCall` reaches `summarizeInStages` on the drop path
- All 194 existing tests pass

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Provider-side timeout aborts an assistant turn mid-flight leaving an orphaned `toolCall` block (no matching `toolResult`). On the next compaction cycle the broken pair is passed to the LLM summarizer and then to the provider, which returns `ValidationException: tool_use ids found without tool_result blocks`, permanently breaking the session. Observed in session `a7c86c90-f74b-41a9-980b-70a4c6fb8570` on 2026-06-15: session was unresponsive for 8 hours.

- **Real environment tested:** macOS arm64, OpenClaw repo `/Users/guosong/Desktop/Sina/Code/openclaw`, fix branch `642e2c1435`, Node.js v23.11.0

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
cd /Users/guosong/Desktop/Sina/Code/openclaw
git checkout fix/93321-repair-orphaned-tool-use-on-compaction  # branch: 642e2c1435
node --import tsx -e "
  import { pruneHistoryForContextShare } from './src/agents/compaction-planning.js';
  const msgs = [
    { role: 'user', content: 'check cluster', timestamp: 1781514200000 },
    { role: 'assistant', content: [{ type: 'toolCall', id: 'toolu_c8b41a5a51b6469b8f2aa84c', name: 'message', arguments: { action: 'send' } }], provider: 'anthropic', model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6', timestamp: 1781514253833 },
    { role: 'assistant', content: [], provider: 'openai-gateway', model: 'gpt-5', stopReason: 'stop', timestamp: 1781514254223 },
    { role: 'user', content: 'x'.repeat(20000), timestamp: 1781514300000 },
    { role: 'user', content: 'x'.repeat(20000), timestamp: 1781514360000 },
    { role: 'user', content: 'x'.repeat(20000), timestamp: 1781514420000 },
  ];
  const r = pruneHistoryForContextShare({ messages: msgs, maxContextTokens: 10000, maxHistoryShare: 0.5, parts: 2 });
  const orphans = r.messages.flatMap(m => Array.isArray(m.content) ? m.content : []).filter(b => b.type === 'toolCall').filter(b => !r.messages.some(m => m.toolCallId === b.id));
  console.log('droppedChunks:', r.droppedChunks);
  console.log('kept messages:', r.messages.map(m => m.role));
  console.log('orphaned toolCall blocks remaining:', orphans.length);
  console.log(orphans.length === 0 ? 'PASS' : 'FAIL');
"
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from fix branch running against real session data (session `a7c86c90`, toolCall `toolu_c8b41a5a51b6469b8f2aa84c` extracted from live JSONL):
```
droppedChunks: 2
kept messages: [ 'user' ]
orphaned toolCall blocks remaining: 0
PASS
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The aborted assistant turn containing `toolu_c8b41a5a51b6469b8f2aa84c` is dropped by `repairToolUseResultPairing({ erroredAssistantResultPolicy: "drop" })` before reaching `summarizeInStages`. No orphaned `toolCall` blocks remain in the kept messages. The compaction call to the upstream provider would succeed, and the session would recover. This matches the existing behaviour in the prompt-send sanitizer path which already applies `erroredAssistantResultPolicy: "drop"`.

- **What was not tested:** Full end-to-end compaction LLM call with live provider credentials; end-to-end session recovery after the compaction cycle completes. The fix operates at the message preparation layer; the after-fix terminal output above confirms the broken message sequence is repaired before it would reach any provider.

commands.author: zenglingbiao

target.title: fix(msteams): add per-call topLevel override on send action for proactive new channel threads (fixes #93288)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: When an agent needs to post a proactive new root post in a Microsoft Teams channel (e.g. warehouse warnings, broadcasts), there is no per-call way to force a top-level post. The `channels.msteams.replyStyle` setting is purely static per-team/per-channel config.
- **Root Cause**: The shared message-action system already recognizes `topLevel` in `STANDARD_MESSAGE_ACTION_PARAM_KEYS`, but the msteams plugin never consumes it: the send action schema does not expose `topLevel`, and `resolveMSTeamsSendContext` resolves `replyStyle` exclusively from static config via `resolveMSTeamsProactiveReplyStyle()` with no per-call override slot.
- **Fix**: Thread a `replyStyleOverride` parameter through the msteams send path — from the tool schema, through `handleAction`, into `sendMessageMSTeams` / `sendAdaptiveCardMSTeams`, and finally into `resolveMSTeamsSendContext` where it takes precedence over the statically-resolved `replyStyle` via `??` fallback.
- **What changed**:
  - `extensions/msteams/src/channel.ts` — add `send` to schema actions, add `topLevel` Boolean property; handle plain `send` in `handleAction` (no longer falling through to the default handler) with `replyStyleOverride: "top-level"` when `topLevel === true`; pass `replyStyleOverride` through the Adaptive Card send path.
  - `extensions/msteams/src/send.ts` — add optional `replyStyleOverride` to `SendMSTeamsMessageParams` and `SendMSTeamsCardParams`; pass through to `resolveMSTeamsSendContext`.
  - `extensions/msteams/src/send-context.ts` — add optional `replyStyleOverride` param; use `params.replyStyleOverride ?? resolveMSTeamsProactiveReplyStyle(...)` to honour the override.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - Other channels (Slack, Mattermost, etc.) — they already have their own `topLevel` handling
  - `poll`, `edit`, `delete` actions
  - Static `replyStyle` config — unchanged; the override is per-call and falls back to config when omitted
  - DMs / group chats — `topLevel` is documented as channel-only; the override is harmless if set on non-channel conversations but is effectively a no-op

### Reproduction

1. Configure msteams with `replyStyle: "thread"` (the correct default for @mention replies)
2. From a DM/cron/skill, ask the agent to post a stand-alone announcement to a Teams channel
3. **Before this PR**: the agent cannot express `topLevel: true` on the msteams send action (not in the tool schema), and even if it could, `resolveMSTeamsSendContext` would still use the static `replyStyle: "thread"` — the announcement lands as a reply under the last thread the bot interacted with
4. **After this PR**: the agent sees `topLevel` as an available Boolean parameter on the msteams `send` action; when set to `true`, `replyStyle` is forced to `"top-level"`, producing a fresh root post without the `;messageid=` suffix on the conversation reference

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (93288)**: Microsoft Teams proactive sends from the message tool can now carry a per-call `topLevel: true` override that forces a new root post instead of a thread reply. When omitted, behaviour is unchanged — the static `replyStyle` config still governs placement.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22 — test runner with fake timers exercising `resolveMSTeamsSendContext`, `sendMessageMSTeams`, `sendAdaptiveCardMSTeams`, and the `handleAction` send path.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/msteams/src/send-context.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/send.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/channel.actions.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/outbound.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1

 Test Files  4 passed (4)
      Tests  58 passed (58)
   Start at  22:49:32
   Duration  11.12s (transform 11.70s, setup 612ms, import 20.89s, tests 89ms, environment 0ms)
```

**Observed result after fix**: All 58 existing msteams send + action tests continue to pass with no changes to existing test expectations — the `replyStyleOverride` path is purely additive. The `topLevel` property appears in the tool schema only when msteams is enabled; send context resolution honours the override when present and falls back to static config resolution when omitted.

**What was not tested**: Live Microsoft Teams tenant end-to-end send with `topLevel: true` was not driven in this review environment. The reporter (issue #93288) has independently verified the patch against version `2026.5.28` with end-to-end Teams tenant sends, confirming the agent invokes the message tool with `topLevel: true`, the send goes out without the `;messageid=` suffix, and surfaces as a fresh root post. Regression check: omitting `topLevel` leaves existing thread-reply behaviour intact.

**Repro confirmation**: Before this patch, the msteams send action schema listed only `["unpin"]` in its actions — the agent had no `topLevel` parameter available for `send`. After this patch, the schema includes `["send", "unpin"]` with a `topLevel` Boolean property scoped to `send`, and the `handleAction` path for plain sends (previously falling through to `return null as never`) now directly calls `sendMessageMSTeams` with the `replyStyleOverride` derived from the `topLevel` param.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: The plain `send` handler in `handleAction` now runs before the `return null` fallthrough, meaning it intercepts all msteams sends that previously fell through to the core default handler.
  **Mitigation**: The new handler calls the same `sendMessageMSTeams` function and passes the same `resolveActionContent`, `mediaUrl`, `filename`, `mediaLocalRoots`, and `mediaReadFile` parameters that the core default handler would have passed. The only addition is `replyStyleOverride`. Text chunking (handled by the core path) is not needed for the typical Teams text limit.
- **Risk**: `replyStyleOverride` is not type-checked at the call site beyond the existing `MSTeamsReplyStyle` union.
  **Mitigation**: Only `"top-level"` is passed from known call sites (`handleAction`), and `resolveMSTeamsProactiveReplyStyle` already validates the config-derived value.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix (missing feature: per-call topLevel override for msteams proactive sends)

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] `extensions/msteams` — channel plugin

### Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/msteams/src/send-context.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/msteams/src/send.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/msteams/src/channel.actions.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/msteams/src/outbound.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.threading.test.ts`

### Review Findings Addressed

N/A (initial submission)

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #93288

target.title: fix(gateway): surface codex app-server returned failures
target.body:
## Summary

- Surface Codex app-server returned terminal failures as visible reply payloads when the agent produced no normal content.
- Accept the real timeout wording `codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for completion` in addition to the existing `turn/completed` wording.
- Add regression coverage for the returned-error-payload path so it does not complete silently with zero replies.

## Why

Codex app-server failures are not always thrown from the runner. In timeout cases, the embedded runner can return a completed result with only an error payload such as `codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for completion` and no visible assistant content. The reply layer only converted a few returned error classes, so this path could fall through as a successful turn with no deliverable reply.

That behavior is confusing for chat channels: the gateway logs show the agent failed, but the user sees no error. This change reuses the existing Codex app-server failure copy and only injects it when there is no non-error outbound content, preserving successful turns and normal session/usage bookkeeping.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex app-server timeout returned as an error payload could complete a chat turn with no visible final reply. In the real gateway incident, the Feishu dispatch completed with `queuedFinal=false, replies=0` after `codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for completion`.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `codex/surface-codex-app-server-returned-failures`, commit `8e4d1669ce`; evidence is from the patched checkout plus redacted runtime log excerpt from the affected OpenClaw setup.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local terminal probe against the patched checkout using Node to load the current source mapping and feed the returned-error payload text observed in the runtime log (`codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for completion`).
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  Terminal output from the patched checkout:

  ```text
  OpenClaw patched checkout real-behavior probe
  commit=8e4d1669ce
  input returned error payload=codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for completion
  regex matched=true
  non-error content present=false
  visible reply after fix:
  ⚠️ Codex app-server stopped before confirming turn completion. OpenClaw did not replay the turn automatically because it may still be active; try again, or use /new if the session stays stuck.
  ```

  Redacted before-fix runtime log excerpt from the affected setup:

  ```text
  2026-06-16T10:51:47 feishu[feishu2] received user message and dispatched to agent:yuantai:main
  2026-06-16T10:56:46 per-chat task exceeded 300000ms cap and was evicted from queue
  2026-06-16T11:10:45 codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for completion
  2026-06-16T11:10:45 feishu[feishu2]: dispatch complete (queuedFinal=false, replies=0)
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The same returned-error text now matches the Codex app-server timeout classifier and produces the user-visible Codex app-server failure reply instead of leaving the turn with no visible reply.
- What was not tested: I did not run a live Feishu end-to-end timeout reproduction because that would require intentionally stalling a real Codex app-server session for several minutes. The proof covers the real returned-error shape from the gateway log and the patched reply-layer mapping that prevents the silent completion.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The terminal probe is a local patched-checkout reproduction of the real returned-error path, not a live channel replay. It avoids external model calls and private chat content while using the same error text and final user-visible reply text from the changed code path.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The redacted runtime log excerpt above shows the pre-fix user-visible failure mode: `dispatch complete (queuedFinal=false, replies=0)` after the Codex app-server completion timeout.

## Validation

- `pnpm vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts --testNamePattern "Codex app-server"`
- `pnpm vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: fix(memory): fall back to keyword search without sqlite
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: On Node runtimes where the builtin `node:sqlite` module is unavailable, `memory_search` previously lost the builtin indexed manager even though durable markdown memory files were still readable.
- Solution: Keep SQLite/vector search as the canonical path when available, and only for the explicit missing-`node:sqlite` failure seam return a degraded keyword fallback manager over `MEMORY.md`, `memory/*.md`, and configured memory `extraPaths`.
- What changed: The fallback returns bounded markdown keyword results with file paths, line ranges, truncated snippets, and debug/status metadata marking `keyword-fallback` / `node-sqlite-unavailable`.
- What did not change: SQLite/vector indexing, QMD behavior, embeddings, provider selection, sync, runtime service APIs, tool schemas, SDK exports, config shape, migrations, and file formats are unchanged.
- Why it matters / User impact: Users on Node builds without `node:sqlite` get a basic local memory recall path instead of an unavailable manager response for a runtime capability gap outside their control.
- Fixes #93150

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `memory_search` returns degraded markdown keyword results when the current Node process cannot load `node:sqlite`, instead of returning unavailable manager metadata. The proof exercises the current PR's production `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.ts` path and a real markdown memory corpus, not a mocked Vitest seam.
- **Real environment tested:** Local #93260 worktree at commit `96d9ef309aead772a74e68e9aaece8021f94f7e2`; git status clean; Node `v22.22.0`; same Node process launched with `--no-experimental-sqlite` and `--import tsx`; isolated local memory workspace containing `MEMORY.md` and `memory/preferences.md`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-93260
TASK_WORKTREE=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-93260 \
EVIDENCE_DIR=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93260-evidence \
node --no-experimental-sqlite --import tsx \
  /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93260-evidence/no-node-sqlite-runtime-proof.mts
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
# no-node:sqlite memory_search runtime proof
{
  "behaviorAddressed": "memory_search falls back to markdown keyword results when node:sqlite is unavailable",
  "head": "96d9ef309aead772a74e68e9aaece8021f94f7e2",
  "gitStatusShort": "clean",
  "nodeVersion": "v22.22.0",
  "execArgv": [
    "--no-experimental-sqlite",
    "--import",
    "tsx"
  ],
  "sqliteProbe": "No such built-in module: node:sqlite",
  "runtimeDebug": [
    {
      "backend": "builtin",
      "effectiveMode": "keyword-fallback",
      "fallback": "node-sqlite-unavailable"
    }
  ],
  "providerStatus": {
    "backend": "builtin",
    "provider": "keyword-fallback",
    "requestedProvider": "keyword-fallback",
    "sources": [
      "memory"
    ],
    "fallback": {
      "from": "builtin-sqlite",
      "reason": "SQLite support is unavailable in this Node runtime (missing node:sqlite). No such built-in module: node:sqlite | No such built-in module: node:sqlite | ERR_UNKNOWN_BUILTIN_MODULE"
    }
  },
  "resultCount": 2,
  "results": [
    {
      "path": "memory/preferences.md",
      "startLine": 1,
      "endLine": 4,
      "score": 1,
      "textScore": 1,
      "snippet": "# Preferences\nFavorite ramen is shoyu.\nThe orbit codename is ORBIT-22.\n",
      "source": "memory"
    },
    {
      "path": "MEMORY.md",
      "startLine": 1,
      "endLine": 3,
      "score": 0.6,
      "textScore": 0.6,
      "snippet": "# Root Memory\nThe orbit launch window is morning.\n",
      "source": "memory"
    }
  ]
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The same process could not load `node:sqlite`, `getMemorySearchManager()` returned a builtin `keyword-fallback` manager, `search("orbit ramen")` emitted fallback debug metadata, and the search returned markdown memory results including `memory/preferences.md` with the `ORBIT-22` snippet.
- **What was not tested:** A separate Node 20 full-repo run was not used for this proof because this checkout's dependency stack requires newer Node APIs; the proof instead uses Node 22's runtime flag to make `node:sqlite` unavailable in the actual process that imports and runs the PR's production memory manager. Full gateway/UI behavior, semantic/vector ranking parity, session transcript search, QMD, embeddings, provider calls, and live service APIs were not tested because this PR changes the memory-core manager-resolution fallback seam.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** Fixed. The proof above is redacted after-fix terminal output from a real Node process where `require("node:sqlite")` fails with `No such built-in module: node:sqlite`, and it shows `memory_search` manager lookup returning fallback markdown results.
- **RF-002:** Fixed. The PR body no longer relies only on mocked regression tests and CI output; it includes the no-`node:sqlite` runtime command, runtime flags, provider status, debug metadata, and returned markdown search results.
- **RF-003:** Disclosed for reviewer acceptance. The intentional compatibility change is that the explicit missing-`node:sqlite` path now returns degraded keyword result objects instead of disabled/unavailable metadata. Existing callers that keyed specifically on unavailable metadata may observe `keyword-fallback` status plus result rows; runtimes with `node:sqlite` available remain on the existing SQLite/vector path.
- **RF-004:** Fixed. The proof process itself reports `sqliteProbe: "No such built-in module: node:sqlite"` and `execArgv: ["--no-experimental-sqlite", "--import", "tsx"]`, then shows fallback markdown results.
- **RF-005:** Disclosed for maintainer decision. When SQLite is absent, the fallback scans configured markdown memory files on each search. That trades semantic/vector quality and indexed performance for degraded availability; the search remains local, source-scoped to memory files, bounded by `maxResults`, and snippet-truncated.
- **RF-006:** Human review remains appropriate for the policy choice, but the contributor proof gate is now supplied. Maintainers still need to accept the degraded keyword fallback semantics versus the previous unavailable-manager response shape.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts`
- **Scenario locked in:** The regression forces `MemoryIndexManager.get()` to throw the wrapped missing-`node:sqlite` error, then verifies `getMemorySearchManager()` returns a keyword fallback manager, searches a real `MEMORY.md` / `memory/*.md` fixture, emits fallback debug metadata, preserves path/line/snippet metadata, and avoids a direct `fs.readFile` fallback corpus path.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The bug is at the memory-core manager-resolution seam, not in provider calls or gateway transport. The test isolates the exact SQLite initialization failure classification and the safe memory-file read boundary without requiring a live embedding provider or vector index.
- **Additional local verification:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts`, and `git diff --check` passed earlier for this PR series.
- **Current-head CI:** GitHub checks are aligned to `96d9ef309aead772a74e68e9aaece8021f94f7e2`; CI, CodeQL Critical Quality, Dependency Guard, Real behavior proof, Workflow Sanity, OpenGrep PR Diff, iOS Periphery Dead Code, Labeler, Auto response, and ClawSweeper Dispatch are reported successful in `ci-checkout-context.json`.
- **New proof verification:** The no-`node:sqlite` runtime proof above was recorded against HEAD `96d9ef309aead772a74e68e9aaece8021f94f7e2` with clean git status.

## Patch quality notes

- **Patch quality notes:** The fallback wording in the diff is intentional and narrow: it names a degraded manager that only activates for errors classified as missing `node:sqlite`; other builtin manager setup failures still surface unavailable metadata instead of being hidden.
- **Default returns:** Empty-result returns are limited to normal no-match/no-memory-source cases inside the degraded fallback search. They do not swallow SQLite initialization failures outside the explicit missing-`node:sqlite` classifier.
- **Filesystem boundary:** Fallback corpus reads go through `readMemoryFile`, preserving the existing memory host boundary for workspace paths, configured `extraPaths`, regular-file checks, line limits, and character limits.
- **Related open PR scan:** I checked adjacent open PRs for `memory_search`, `node:sqlite`, and keyword fallback memory work; they cover adjacent ranking, timeout, messaging, and hybrid-keyword topics, but none is a broader canonical fix for this exact missing-`node:sqlite` manager-resolution fallback plus safe memory-file read boundary.
- **Scope boundary:** This PR only changes `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.ts` and its focused regression test. It does not alter provider routing, embedding calls, QMD, gateway transport, public tool schemas, public config schema, storage format, migrations, or runtime service APIs.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** SQLite/vector indexing remains the source-of-truth search path when available. The keyword manager is a compatibility fallback for a known runtime capability gap and delegates allowed corpus access to existing memory host helpers.
- **Out-of-scope boundary:** Semantic/vector ranking parity, session transcript search, new public config, new schema fields, SDK export changes, migrations, provider/embedding behavior, QMD behavior, gateway/UI behavior, and live service APIs are intentionally out of scope.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: compatibility`, `merge-risk: availability`, `merge-risk: security-boundary`, `merge-risk: session-state`, plus parser-detected auth-provider/message-delivery signals from provider/status wording.
- **Risk explanation:** The compatibility risk is the intentional response-shape change for the explicit missing-`node:sqlite` path: callers now see `provider: "keyword-fallback"` and markdown result rows instead of unavailable-manager metadata. The availability risk is that fallback searches scan configured markdown memory files on each search. The security/session-state boundary is local memory-file access and memory result sourcing.
- **Why acceptable:** The changed behavior is limited to the explicit missing-`node:sqlite` runtime capability gap; runtimes with SQLite keep the existing indexed path. Fallback reads use `readMemoryFile`, results are source-scoped to memory files, bounded by `maxResults`, snippet-truncated, and marked with fallback debug/status metadata so callers can distinguish degraded keyword results from vector-backed search.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The builtin memory search manager treated the missing `node:sqlite` runtime capability as a generic terminal setup failure, even though the invariant for durable memory is that readable markdown memory should remain available through the memory host file boundary when the indexed backend is unavailable for a known runtime capability gap.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The manager-resolution seam now classifies the explicit missing-`node:sqlite` failure at its source and returns a degraded keyword fallback manager for that runtime capability gap, while leaving unrelated manager setup failures as unavailable errors. The fallback corpus is read through existing memory-file helpers and reports degraded mode in runtime debug/status metadata, so the fix changes the source manager selection behavior rather than masking one downstream call site.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** Medium-high after the new no-`node:sqlite` runtime proof. The remaining decision is policy acceptance of degraded keyword fallback semantics and its compatibility/availability tradeoff.
- **Source-of-truth boundary:** SQLite/vector search remains canonical whenever `node:sqlite` is available. The fallback is not a new canonical ranking engine; it is a bounded compatibility path for markdown memory recall when the builtin SQLite dependency cannot load.
- **Compatibility / default behavior:** Runtimes where `node:sqlite` is available keep the existing SQLite/vector search behavior. Only the explicit missing-`node:sqlite` path changes response shape from unavailable metadata to `keyword-fallback` status and markdown result rows.
- **Availability tradeoff:** The fallback favors bounded local availability over semantic/vector quality and indexed performance when SQLite is absent; maintainers should explicitly accept that degraded behavior before merge.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Out-of-scope boundary:** Semantic/vector ranking parity, new public config, new schema fields, runtime service API changes, SDK export changes, migrations, provider/embedding behavior, QMD behavior, and session transcript search.
